ingredients.name: Kichijoji Station
ingredients.address: Kichijoji Minamicho, Musashino, Tokyo, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Kichijoji Station serves as the central hub of Kadode and Ontan's daily world, a place fans recognize as the backdrop for countless casual hangouts and the starting point of their everyday life under the looming mothership. The girls' carefree bond is most alive whenever they exit here and head off to explore their neighborhood.

ingredients.name: FamilyMart Shimokitazawa Station South
ingredients.localName: ファミリーマート 下北沢駅南店
ingredients.type: コンビニエンスストア
categories: コンビニエンスストア
ingredients.address: 2 Chome-14-8 Kitazawa, Setagaya City, Tokyo 155-0031, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Shimokitazawa FamilyMart appears in street-level scenes capturing the girls roaming the eclectic neighborhood, convenience store runs being a small ritual of their ordinary high-school life. Shimokitazawa's indie, youthful atmosphere mirrors the show's tone of normalcy persisting under catastrophe.

ingredients.name: Kannana hekiga
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒155-0033 Tokyo, Setagaya City, Daita, 4 Chome−1
ingredients.entityContext: The Route 7 (Kannana-dori) mural wall is a recognizable visual landmark in the anime's Setagaya street scenes, used to establish the lived-in urban texture of the girls' commute routes. The mural's presence grounds the story firmly in real Tokyo.

ingredients.name: Kichijoji Station North Exit Front Plaza
ingredients.localName: 吉祥寺駅北口駅前広場
ingredients.address: 1 Chome-15 Kichijoji Honcho, Musashino, Tokyo 180-0004, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The plaza in front of Kichijoji's north exit is where Kadode and Ontan's slice-of-life antics frequently play out, framed against the ever-present alien ship overhead. The contrast between normal teen chatter and the surreal sky is one of the anime's defining visual moods.

ingredients.address: Nippon Life Shinjuku West Exit Building, 2F, 1 Chome-17-1 Nishishinjuku, Shinjuku City, Tokyo 160-0023, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Renoir coffee shop near Shinjuku West Exit appears in scenes involving adult characters in the anime, its old-fashioned kissaten interior lending a lived-in, slightly weary quality to conversations that take place there. The Shinjuku West Exit area features in the more urban, adult-oriented storyline threads.

ingredients.entityContext: Kitazawa Junior High School in Shimokitazawa stands in for the kind of institutional school background visible in the characters' neighbourhood scenes, consistent with the anime's Setagaya-Shimokitazawa location cluster. It is part of the documented walking route fans use to retrace the story's geography.

ingredients.entityContext: Nishi-Eifuku Station and its quiet residential streets feature in the anime's quieter neighborhood scenes, part of the Suginami-Setagaya corridor that defines the show's Tokyo. The small station captures the calm suburban texture the series uses to contrast with its apocalyptic premise.

ingredients.entityContext: Yuzuri Bridge over the Kanda River is a documented background location in the anime's Setagaya scenes, part of the canal-side streets the girls walk through in quieter episodes. The small bridge's riverside setting is a modest but confirmed pilgrimage spot.

ingredients.entityContext: Shimo-takaido Station and its surrounding shotengai (shopping street) appear in the anime as part of the retro, low-rise Tokyo streetscape that Kadode and Ontan inhabit. The area's showa-era atmosphere reinforces the series' theme of ordinary life carrying on beneath the alien occupation.

ingredients.name: Dream Bridge
ingredients.address: Tadehara, Fuji, Shizuoka 416-0931, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Yume-no-Ohashi (Dream Bridge) in Odaiba appears in a pivotal emotional sequence, with the waterfront setting giving the scene an open, melancholic quality that stands out against the confined urban world the characters usually inhabit. The bridge is one of the more visually distinct location-shots in the anime.

ingredients.entityContext: Iwa Beach in Kanagawa appears during an emotionally charged getaway scene, one of the rare moments where the characters leave Tokyo and encounter the coast as a space of temporary escape and reflection. The beach sequence is memorable for its quiet shift in tone.

ingredients.entityContext: Daitabashi Station on the Keio Line appears repeatedly as a transit point in the characters' neighborhood wanderings, with its quiet Setagaya streetscape lending the series its unhurried, everyday atmosphere. Fans trace Kadode and Ontan's routes through this small station.
